implementationArtifact.specBody:
---
complexity: low
---

# Feature: Add Footer

## Summary

Add a simple footer to the bottom of every page with copyright text.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Footer appears at the bottom of the page
- [ ] Footer contains copyright text with the current year and "BrewBoard"

## Technical Approach

Add a footer element to `src/app/page.tsx`. One line, no complex styling needed.

## Out of Scope

- Do NOT write tests
- Do NOT add documentation
- Do NOT refactor existing code

## Validation

```bash
echo "Feature Add Footer validated"
```

quality.summary: Near-perfect implementation of a low-complexity feature. Both acceptance criteria are satisfied exactly — a semantic <footer> with dynamic year and 'BrewBoard' text is appended after the beer cards. The change is surgical (3 lines), uses correct Tailwind utility classes consistent with the project, and introduces no regressions. Minor deduction: placing <footer> inside <main> is semantically debatable (a page-level footer is conventionally a sibling of <main>, not a child), though it is harmless and the spec explicitly said 'Add a footer element to src/app/page.tsx' without prescribing placement depth. Log file is a blank workflow artifact — acceptable for a low-complexity one-liner.

requirements.notes: Both acceptance criteria met verbatim: footer appears at page bottom, contains '© <currentYear> BrewBoard'. No tests written, no docs added, no refactoring performed. Out-of-scope items cleanly avoided.

correctness.notes: Valid JSX, correct © entity, new Date().getFullYear() is the idiomatic runtime year expression, Tailwind classes are syntactically valid. No type errors or logic issues.

minimality.notes: Exactly 3 lines added to page.tsx. The log file is a workflow artifact, not churn. Zero unnecessary changes.

code_quality.notes: Semantic <footer> element used (good), Tailwind utilities match project style (mt-8 text-center text-sm text-stone-500 are appropriate). Tiny knock: <footer> inside <main> is valid but slightly non-standard for a page-level footer; a strict HTML purist would place it as a sibling of <main>.

risk.notes: No regressions possible with a 3-line additive change. Dynamic year avoids stale copyright text. The footer-inside-main placement is the only minor semantic concern but carries no accessibility or functional risk.
